Health_Department_Name: Sonoma County Department of Health Services
Program_Description: Provides free birth control and other health services to eligible community members.
Application_Process: Can help you sign up for Family Pact at Sonoma County Health Clinics.

Health_Department_Name: Sonoma County Department of Health Services
Program_Description: Helps keep you from getting pregnant if you had sex without using birth control, or if your birth control method did not work.

Health_Department_Name: Sonoma County Department of Health Services
Program_Description: Offers education about birth control, abortion, and sexual health and well-being.

Health_Department_Name: Sonoma County Department of Health Services
Program_Description: The Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program (CPSP) provides supportive and educational services for pregnant women with Medi-Cal coverage, from conception through 60 days postpartum.

Health_Department_Name: Sonoma County Department of Health Services
Program_Name: Sonoma County Perinatal Mental Health Toolkit
Program_Description: The Sonoma County Perinatal Mental Health Toolkit begins with preventive interventions and ends with educational resources to underscore the importance and effectiveness of education and prevention to reduce the risk of perinatal anxiety and depression on mothers, fathers or other caregivers, and their infants.
Target_Population: Pregnant and postpartum individuals experiencing mental health challenges

Health_Department_Name: Sonoma County Department of Health Services
Program_Name: Pregnancy and Substance Use, a Harm Reduction Toolkit
Program_Description: This resource was created to promote the overall health and wellbeing of pregnant and parenting people who use drugs, their loved ones, and their service providers.
Target_Population: Pregnant and parenting people who use drugs

Health_Department_Name: Sonoma County Department of Health Services
Program_Name: Opioid Use Disorder Toolkit: Supporting the Public Health Response in Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health
Program_Description: ASTHO, with support from the CDC National Center for Injury Prevention and Control, created this toolkit to train public health and maternal, child, and adolescent health partners on prevention strategies and community resources regarding the effects of substance use disorder on reproductive-aged women, pregnant women, and their infants.
Target_Population: Reproductive-aged women, pregnant women, and their infants
